stories.title: Texas Volts Snap 4-Game Skid — Rachel Garcia CG, Volts Beat Bandits 5-3
stories.angle: The Texas Volts beat the Chicago Bandits 5-3 on Monday night, with Rachel Garcia throwing her third complete game of the season to snap a four-game losing streak. Volts are now 5-8 with 13 days left in the regular season. The regular season ends July 20, the play-in game is July 23, and the Championship Series runs July 25-27. With every game in the final two weeks critical, last night's result keeps the Volts' playoff hopes alive — and gives NiJaree Canady time and space to continue her mechanics reset with Tori Nyberg.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Rachel Garcia threw her third complete game of the season Monday night.

Texas Volts 5, Chicago Bandits 3.

Four-game losing streak: over. Volts are 5-8 with 13 days left in the regular season.

#Softball #AUSL #TexasVolts

x_posts.text:

#Softball #AUSL

stories.title: FSU 2027 — The Quiet Rebuild Taking Shape
stories.angle: FSU lost Isa Torres and Jaysoni Beachum to the transfer portal, but the Seminoles are quietly assembling a dangerous 2027 roster. Pitcher Jazzy Francik (ACC POY) and outfielder Ashtyn Danley return. Ella Dodge (11 HR, 46 RBI at Tennessee) and Nicole Edmiaston (23 HR, .900 SLG at Stetson) arrived via portal. Incoming class is led by five-star infielder McLanie Hudson (Softball America No. 5 nationally). With 5 of 6 incoming class players rated high and two All-Americans returning, the Seminoles are a legitimate 2027 contender being underestimated.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
FSU 2027 doesn't look like a rebuild from the inside.

Francik (ACC POY) + Danley (All-American) return.
Dodge (11 HR, 46 RBI) + Edmiaston (23 HR, .900 SLG) arrive.
Hudson (SA No. 5 nationally) incoming.

Don't sleep on the Seminoles.

#Softball #FSU

- Ella Dodge: infielder, from Tennessee; 11 HR, 46 RBI in 2026; started all 61 games; hit .265
- Nicole Edmiaston: from Stetson; .453 BA, .900 SLG, 23 HR, 70 RBI; school record
- Incoming class led by McLanie Hudson (5-star, Softball America No. 5 nationally)
- Class of 6 players: McLanie Hudson (INF), Addyson Ware (INF/OF), Kaylee Goodpaster (OF), Callee Leffler (OF), Ava Lorenzatti (P), Missy Odom (P)
- FSU's class ranked No. 13 nationally
- Isa Torres and Jaysoni Beachum left via portal (covered in prior sessions)
**Freshness:** Most recent roster news; FSU class finalized; forward-looking 2027 preview.

stories.title: Megan Grant ESPYS — 8 Days Left to Vote
stories.angle: Eight days remain to vote for Megan Grant (Best Record-Breaking Performance, July 15 ESPYS, ABC). Grant's 42-HR season shattered the 31-year-old NCAA record. Her competition includes NFL star Myles Garrett, Norwegian cross-country skier Johannes Høsflot Klæbo, and marathon runner Sabastian Sawe. Those are sports with enormous, mainstream global followings. Winning would be an upset — which is exactly why every softball fan needs to vote every single day. Vote at ESPN.com before July 15 at 8 PM ET on ABC.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Megan Grant: 42 HRs this season, breaking a 31-year NCAA record.

Up for Best Record-Breaking Performance at the ESPYS on July 15.

Competing against Myles Garrett, an Olympic skier, and a marathon runner.

8 days left. Vote at ESPN.com.

#Softball #ESPYS

stories.title: Garcia Wins, Canady Waits — The Volts' Two-Pitcher Plan
stories.angle: When Rachel Garcia throws a complete game and beats the Bandits, it does two things: it gives the Texas Volts (5-8) a fighting chance in the standings, and it gives NiJaree Canady one more day to work on her mechanics with Tori Nyberg away from game pressure. Garcia, a Stanford alum herself, is holding the roster together while Canady's reset continues. The Volts need Canady — 13 days remain, and her window to show what she can do before the Championship Series is closing.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Rachel Garcia: 3 complete games this AUSL season.
NiJaree Canady: mechanics reset ongoing with Tori Nyberg.
Texas Volts: 5-8, still in it.

Garcia's winning while Canady rebuilds. That's how the Volts stay alive with 13 days left.

#Softball #AUSL #TexasVolts

research.facts:
Garcia Wins, Canady Waits — The Volts' Two-Pitcher Plan
**Sources:**
- AUSL game result (July 6 Volts 5-3 Bandits, Rachel Garcia CG — confirmed)
- SI.com: "Why the Texas Volts Called Stanford to Help NiJaree Canady" (Tori Nyberg, mechanics work)
- Prior pipeline coverage: Canady 0-4, 6.87 ERA confirmed as of July 6
**Key Verified Facts (MEDIUM):**
- Rachel Garcia: Stanford alum; 3rd complete game of 2026 AUSL season
- NiJaree Canady: 0-4, 6.87 ERA; mechanics work ongoing with Tori Nyberg
- Nyberg identified Canady's "squared up" position had reverted to "open" position after injuries at Texas Tech
- Goal: return Canady to "OG NiJa" — her dominant Stanford form
- AUSL regular season ends July 20 (13 days from July 7)
- Volts now 5-8 — still mathematically alive in playoff race

stories.title: PGF Nationals 12 Days — Roster Freeze in 6
stories.angle: PGF Nationals is 12 days away (July 19, Great Park Irvine). The roster freeze deadline hits in 6 days — July 13. With the transfer portal contact window open through July 31 and the September 1 contact window 56 days out, coaches will head to Irvine to evaluate the 2028 class while the 2027 roster-building window for most programs has largely closed. It's the moment the sport's future gets built in real time.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
PGF Nationals: 12 days away.
Roster freeze: 6 days away (July 13).
September 1 contact window: 56 days away.

The portal closed. The showcase season opens.

#Softball #PGFNationals #CollegeSoftball
